---
title: "Structured Illumination Electron Ptychography at the Atomic Scale"
type: "article"
author: "P. Pelz, H. DeVyldere, P. Ercius, M. Scott"
year: "2022"
publication: "Microscopy and Microanalysis 28, 388-390"
preprint: ""
doi: "10.1017/s1431927622002288"
materials: ""
code_url: ""
project_page_url: ""
toc: false
categories:
  - ptychography
  - atomic resolution
# --- citation metrics (auto-managed; do not edit) ---
cited_by_count: 4
fwci: 1.06
citation_percentile: 74.7
is_oa: true
oa_status: bronze
counts_by_year:
  - [2017, 0]
  - [2018, 0]
  - [2019, 0]
  - [2020, 0]
  - [2021, 0]
  - [2022, 0]
  - [2023, 3]
  - [2024, 1]
keywords:
  - "Atomic units"
  - "Ptychography"
metrics_updated: "2026-05-02T16:35:20Z"
# --- end citation metrics ---
---
## Citation (APA 7)

> Structured Illumination Electron Ptychography at the Atomic Scale
P. Pelz, H. DeVyldere, P. Ercius, M. Scott
Microscopy and Microanalysis 28, 388-390


## Abstract

With the advent of fast direct electron detectors, electron ptychography is becoming increasingly popular as a high-resolution, high-sensitivity phase-contrast method in electron microscopy. Electron ptychography is a computational phase-contrast imaging method that reconstructs phase-contrast images from scanning diffraction measurements, acquired by raster-scanning a spatially confined beam over the field of view.
